exchanges.text: I am Patrick Kirkland: writer, creative director, and occasional narrative mechanic for brands that have too many thoughts and not enough shape. I have spent 20-plus years making campaigns, films, launches, decks, speeches, and strategy behave like they are all part of the same story. The useful version: I help people say the thing they mean before the room invents a safer sentence.
what do you actually do day-to-day?

I write the line, the argument under the line, and the deck that gets the line out of a conference room alive. Some days that means campaign concepts and copy. Some days it means positioning, founder narrative, naming, film scripts, launch language, or pulling a messy brief apart until the real problem admits it exists.
what's it like working with you?

Direct, collaborative, and allergic to theater. I like a strong point of view, a clear owner, and enough room to make the first draft useful instead of decorative. I will push back when the brief is ducking the real question, and I will take a good note fast. I do not need the work to be precious. I need it to be true enough to survive contact with people.
why is the writing shelf so empty?

Because most writing samples either violate an NDA, require eleven minutes of context, or make the writer look like someone who says 'thought leadership' without flinching. The shelf will fill in. For now, ask me for a sample and I will send something that matches the kind of problem you actually have.

Yes, selectively. I am best for founders, arts organizations, mission-driven brands, and agencies that need senior thinking without turning the whole assignment into a procurement ritual. Email works better than a form: patrick@pkthewriter.com.
